214.title: Odds to Make the NFL Playoffs | FanDuel Sportsbook
214.description: Predict NFL playoff contenders with odds to make the postseason at FanDuel Sportsbook. Back your favorite teams and celebrate their success!
214.footer:
Sportsbook Odds / NFL Odds / NFL Playoffs Odds

Odds to Make NFL Playoffs

Bet on which NFL teams are going to make it to the NFL Playoffs with FanDuel Sportsbook.

The Buffalo Bills lead the way as the favorites to make the AFC Playoffs at -340, followed closely by the Baltimore Ravens (-330) and the New England Patriots (-215). In the NFC, the Los Angeles Rams sit atop the board at -335, pursued by the Seattle Seahawks (-260) and the Detroit Lions (-200).

On the flipside, we have the odds to miss the playoffs for teams staring at extremely long playoff odds. The Miami Dolphins (-2500), New York Jets (-1350), and Cleveland Browns (-950) headline the teams most likely to miss the AFC Playoffs, while the Arizona Cardinals (-4500), New York Giants (-355), and Carolina Panthers (-270) lead the NFC miss market. Check back throughout the 2026-27 season to see how trades, injuries, and performance shift these playoff futures.

How NFL Playoff Odds Work - Reading the “Make or Miss” Market

FanDuel Sportsbook posts two-way markets on every NFL club: Odds to Make the Playoffs and Odds to Miss the Playoffs. These are moneyline prices, so a minus sign (-) shows how much you must stake to win $100, while a plus sign (+) shows the profit on a $100 wager. Playoff odds open moments after the draft and can move after a single marquee signing or major injury. Setting up alerts on NFL news lets you pounce when numbers drift.

As an example, if you bet $160 on the Tennessee Titans to make the playoffs at -160 odds, that bet would pay out $260 (your original $160 stake and $100 in winnings). If you wagered $100 on the Miami Dolphins to make the playoffs at +118 odds, you would earn $218 (your original $100 bet and $118 in winnings).


NFL Playoff Futures FAQs


  • When does my Make Playoff or Miss Playoff bet settle?

    FanDuel settles the market as soon as the league publishes its final postseason field using the NFL’s own tiebreakers. Subsequent league appeals or statistical revisions don’t change the payout.

  • Can I cash out my playoff futures early?

    Often, yes. When the Cash-Out button appears, you can lock in a profit or cut losses instantly. The request isn’t guaranteed (odds can move or the market can suspend) but if it’s accepted, the offer shown is final and your bet is settled on the spot.

  • Can I combine “Make the Playoffs” odds in a parlay?

    Sure, but remember parlay rules still apply. If one leg is void (say, a team becomes ineligible because the format changes), that leg drops out and the parlay is re-priced on the remaining selections. If multiple legs become correlated because of a schedule change, FanDuel may void or resettle the ticket to keep things fair.

336.title: Super Bowl Odds 2027 - NFL Championship Betting | FanDuel
336.description: Bet on 2027 NFL Super Bowl Odds with FanDuel Sportsbook. Find odds for each team to win the Lombardi Trophy. Join now and place your bets with FanDuel!
336.footer:
Sportsbook Odds / NFL Odds / Super Bowl Odds

Super Bowl 2027 Betting - Super Bowl Odds, Spreads, Moneylines, and Props

The NFL season is here and FanDuel Sportsbook has all the odds you need to bet on Super Bowl 61. Find the latest online sportsbook odds for the 2027 Super Bowl game here at FanDuel Sportsbook. As the season progresses and the matchup is determined, you can bet on the who will win the Super Bowl in 2026 and how each quarter will play out, whether that's through the spread or moneyline. You can also bet on Super Bowl prop bets such as player props, game props, and fun novelty props. At FanDuel Sportsbook, you have more ways to win when betting on the Super Bowl.

2027 Super Bowl Odds to Win

Fresh off hosting Super Bowl LXI on their home turf, the Los Angeles Rams open as the +700 favorites to win Super Bowl LXII. They are closely pursued by the defending champion Seattle Seahawks (+950), looking to repeat back-to-back titles. Rounding out the top contenders are the Buffalo Bills (+1000) and Baltimore Ravens (+1100). Check back throughout the 2026-27 season to see how trades, injuries, and performance impact these championship futures.

Super Bowl Point Spreads and Lines

Once we know which two NFL teams will play in Super Bowl 61, FanDuel Sportsbook has officially released NFL game lines and point spreads for the big game. These odds are similar to the all NFL odds seen during the regular season and throughout the NFL Playoffs.

Any favorite’s point spread is displayed with a “minus” symbol next to a whole number or a halved fraction, such as (-6) or (-6.5). Bettors picking the favorite “against the spread” must hope that the team wins by more than 6 points – a 1 through 6-point victory or a straight-up loss loses the wager. An underdog’s point spread is displayed with a “+” symbol, indicating the number of points “spotted” to the team before the Super Bowl kicks off. Underdog point-spread gamblers are hoping that the NFL team will “cover” by winning by any amount or losing by any amount smaller than the betting line. If the underdog’s point spread is (+6) and the team loses by 5 points, the bettors still win the payout.

If a game finishes with the favorites winning by the exact line margin shown on the point spread, bets are returned in an outcome known as a “push.”


Reading Super Bowl Moneyline Odds

Moneyline odds promise a % payoff when a sports bettor's chosen team wins the game straight-up. A favorite’s moneyline NFL odds are denoted with a “-“ as in (-200). Underdog moneylines are marked with a “+” as in a ML of (+350). Payoff is calculated differently for the betting odds in the favorite’s and underdog’s markets, but always centered around the value of $100.

For instance, (-150) Super Bowl odds mean that for every $150 wagered on a team, the house will pay out $100 if that team wins. (-200) odds stand for $100 payoff on every $200 wagered at the betting book. On the underdog side, moneyline odds represent the amount paid out on won $100 bets if the team surprises. (+350) odds equal a $350 payout on $100, (+1000) odds represent $1000 payout on the same risk, and so on.

    Yes. FanDuel grades almost every NFL game market—including full-game spreads, moneylines, and over/unders—on the final official score after all overtime periods are completed, unless the market header specifically says otherwise.

  • What happens if an NFL game ends in a tie?

    If the final score is tied after overtime, moneyline wagers are void and stakes are refunded, while point-spread and totals bets are settled on the posted numbers (so a spread that lands exactly on the closing line is graded as a push).

  • What happens if an NFL game is postponed or moved to a different stadium?

    When a game is postponed before kickoff, all wagers are void. If it’s relocated but still played on the scheduled date—whether to a neutral site or the opponent’s stadium—bets remain live because FanDuel grades action on the official league box score, not the venue.

  • How does FanDuel treat a Same Game Parlay if one NFL leg is void (for example, because the game ended in a tie)?

    If a leg is voided, the parlay is re-priced to the odds of the remaining legs and still stands. Only when every leg in the SGP is void does FanDuel return the entire stake.

ANY_TIME_TOUCHDOWN_SCORER: Anytime TD: A touchdown scorer is a player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone, not the QB or player who passes the ball.
This is an all-in market. If no team scores a touchdown, individual players & team defense selections will be settled as a loss.
Please see Game Specials for the no touchdown scorer selection.
Defense selection does not include Special Teams.
1ST_TEAM_TOUCHDOWN_SCORER: The winner is the player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone for the respective team's first TD (not the QB or player who passes the ball).
Defense selection does not include Special Teams.
This is an all-in market. If the respective team does not score a touchdown, individual players & team defense selections will be settled as a loss.
ANYTIME_1ST_HALF_TD_SCORER: The winner is the player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone during the 1st half, not the QB or player who passes the ball.
This is an all-in market. If no team scores a touchdown in the 1st half, individual players & team defense selections will be settled as a loss.
Defense selection does not include Special Teams.
ANYTIME_2ND_HALF_TD_SCORER: The winner is the player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone during the 2nd half (including Overtime).
This is an all-in market. If no team scores a touchdown in the 2nd half, individual players & team defense selections will be settled as a loss.
Defense selection does not include Special Teams.
X_TOUCHDOWN_SCORER: The winner is the player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone (not the QB or player who passes the ball).
This is an all-in market - if no team scores a touchdown, individual players & team defense selections will be settled as a loss.
1ST_HALF_WINNER_THREE_WAY: If the score is a tie at the end of the First Half, the winning wager would be "Tie"
1ST_QUARTER_WINNER_THREE_WAY: If the score is a tie at the end of the First Quarter, the winning wager would be "Tie"
2ND_QUARTER_WINNER_THREE_WAY: If both teams score the same number of points within the 2nd Quarter, the winning bet is "Tie"
3RD_QUARTER_WINNER_THREE_WAY: If both teams score the same number of points within the 3rd Quarter, the winning bet is "Tie"
4TH_QUARTER_WINNER_THREE_WAY: If both teams score the same number of points within the 4th Quarter, the winning bet is "Tie"
TO_SCORE_2+_TOUCHDOWNS: The touchdown scorer is the player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone, not the QB who passes the ball.
Defense selection does not include Special Teams.
TO_SCORE_3+_TOUCHDOWNS: The touchdown scorer is the player who carries or receives the ball in the end zone, not the QB who passes the ball.
